基于全球碳中和未来的目标，核能的扩展是满足不断增长的电力需求同时减少环境影响的重要选择。为了使核能更加可持续和安全，闭合燃料循环至关重要，其中乏燃料（SNF）的再处理是关键。几十年来，各种工艺被开发出来以提取用于后续利用和处置的放射性核素，包括PUREX（钚-铀萃取）、UNEX（通用萃取）、FPEX（裂变产物萃取）等。这些工艺涉及使用不同的溶剂分子选择性地与液体混合物中的特定阳离子结合，该混合物通常高度腐蚀性且具有放射性。因此，系统研究配体与这些工艺中涉及的阳离子之间的配位化学可以显著推动乏燃料再处理技术和核废料管理策略的发展。

This phenomenon is called algorithmic homogeneity or model monoculture. It refers to a situation where artificial intelligence systems share similar architectures, training data, or optimization methods due to widespread adoption of specific frameworks or approaches. When AI models are developed with uniform structures or datasets, their decision-making processes can become narrow and predictable. This can make them more susceptible to adversarial attacks, biases, and systemic failures. In the case of machine learning systems, algorithmic homogeneity has led to vulnerabilities where coordinated attacks exploit shared weaknesses across models. Algorithmic homogeneity can have serious consequences for security and reliability, which is why many experts advocate for diverse model designs and robust testing protocols.

The proportional gain (KP), integral gain (KI), and derivative gain (KD) in a PID controller each impact the system differently. KP affects the system's immediate response and reduces steady-state error but can lead to overshoot and instability if too high. KI addresses steady-state error but may introduce instability and overshoot. KD minimizes overshoot and dampens oscillations, improving stability, though excessive KD can slow the system's response. Balancing these gains is essential for optimal performance.

Rank preserving structural failure time model (RPSFTM) adjusts survival times by estimating what they would have been without treatment switching. It uses counterfactual survival times based on the treatment effect and observed data. For example, in an oncology trial, if patients switch from control to treatment, RPSFTM recalculates survival times as if they had stayed in the control group, preserving the rank order of survival outcomes.
